resources.string: Host (primary)\nInstall the project Magisk module so this app runs as a priv-app with TETHER_PRIVILEGED.\nReliable remote hotspot control on many ROMs (including stock/OEM) usually needs this. Root alone is often not enough for stable TetheringManager and shell behavior.
Keep this device nearby and enable pairing mode only when needed. Use the Magisk module on the host (see above).
Use these controls from tablet/secondary phone to wake hotspot remotely.
Already paired. Re-pair only if you rotated host secret.
Manual pairing secret applied. You can send commands now.
Pairing started. Compare codes on both devices.
Pairing start failed. Keep host nearby and in host mode.
Host service: ON, Pairing mode: ON
Host service: ON, Pairing mode: OFF
Host found, but pairing mode is OFF. Enable it on host.
Host not found. Keep host app open in Host mode and nearby.
Bluetooth is off on controller device.
Host found but handshake failed. Ensure both apps are updated and retry.
Root status: DENIED (grant `su` to this app)
Client status: Paired, host not reachable
After sync, a snapshot of host tether / soft AP info shows here. Keep the host nearby and use Sync below.
Could not fetch hotspot settings from host
The paired controller will be cleared and a new host secret will be created. The tablet or secondary device must pair again before sending commands.
This device will forget the host pairing. Run nearby pairing (or enter a new secret) to connect again.
Host pairing cleared. New secret is shown above.
Control the hotspot from another device

The phone that has mobile data runs in Host mode. Your tablet (or a second phone) uses Controller mode over Bluetooth to turn the access point on or off without opening settings on the host. The host should use the project Magisk module for reliable TETHER_PRIVILEGED tethering on many ROMs.
I am the main phone (hotspot + SIM / data plan)
I am the tablet or secondary device
On this phone: keep Host mode, enable pairing when needed, and approve the code from the other device. On the controller: start nearby pairing and match the short code.
Put the host phone near this device, open the host in Host mode, then use Start nearby pairing and confirm the code. You can also paste the host’s secret and save.
The hotspot is driven on this device. You can add a tile or widget after this walkthrough, or use the full console (⋮) for logs and more.
Other devices should join this phone’s access point. Use the full console to see the latest AP / tether status.
Not connected to Wi‑Fi (or Wi‑Fi off)
On Wi‑Fi, but SSID is hidden. Grant location and stay near the access point to see the name.
Add the Quick Settings tile to toggle the hotspot in one tap. Add the home screen widget for the same. Some launchers need you to add tiles by editing the quick settings panel.
If the system supports it, a prompt appears to add the tile. Otherwise, edit the quick settings panel and drag the tile in.
If your launcher allows it, you can pin the widget. Otherwise, long-press the home screen → Widgets → Instant Hotspot.
Add the tile from the quick settings panel (Edit) on this Android version.
Synced. SSID: %1$s Password: %2$s. Opening Wi-Fi settings.
Synced, but SSID/password were not found in host output.
